warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity (electrophilic f⁺, Mulliken charges) of pyridine was evaluated at B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier “standard”, density-fitted DFT) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Neutral geometry was read from the supplied XYZ and kept fixed; cation and anion single-points were run at the same coordinates to obtain f⁺, f⁻, and the dual descriptor (property//geometry B3LYP/def2-TZVP//XYZ). The headline value is the largest f⁺ component, located on the pyridine N atom.

Files: live engine log streamed to the run directory (…pyridine_fukui_reactivity.out); result JSON at …/live.json. Calculation converged; integrity checks passed. Warnings above reflect normal fukui charge-conservation diagnostics.

Method provenance block:
• Property — Fukui indices f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor (Mulliken)
• Level — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI-JK density fitting)
• Geometry — supplied XYZ (no re-optimization)
• Solvent — gas phase (implicit none)
• Charge/multiplicity — 0/1 (neutral), +1/2 (cation), −1/2 (anion)
• Software — PySCF via chemkit vX.Y
• Convergence — all SCF cycles converged; n_steps ≤ 50 per state; no fallbacks
